steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between the noun 'song' and the title '"A Tear" by Gene McDaniels.' The title directly identifies which song — it is a restrictive appositive, not extra information. The correct punctuation must connect a noun to its restrictive appositive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘song:’: A colon introduces a list or elaboration after an independent clause. 'Hit' followed by a title is not an elaboration of a complete independent clause here. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘song;’: A semicolon separates two independent clauses. What follows the blank is a title, not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘song,’: A comma before an appositive signals non-restrictive (extra) information. But the title is necessary to specify which song, so it is restrictive and takes no comma. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘song’: No punctuation is correct before a restrictive appositive that immediately identifies its noun. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
